generated_headline: BARCLAYS PAID $361 MILLION FOR MANIPULATING HOW MUCH MONEY IT WAS RAISING DURING THE 2008 CRISIS — THEN PAID $2.4 BILLION FOR MORTGAGE SECURITIES FRAUD — THEN BECAME EUROPE'S LARGEST FOSSIL FUEL FINANCIER
executive_summary: Barclays PLC is the UK's third-largest bank and one of the world's largest investment banks. Barclays paid $361 million to the DOJ in 2016 to resolve allegations that it manipulated the price of its own securities during a 2008 fundraising from Qatar and Abu Dhabi. Barclays paid $2 billion to the DOJ in 2016 for mortgage-backed securities fraud. The bank was the first major bank fined for LIBOR manipulation in 2012 — paying $290 million to US and UK regulators. Rainforest Action Network's Banking on Climate Chaos report has consistently ranked Barclays as the largest European financier of fossil fuel expansion globally, having provided approximately $167 billion in fossil fuel financing since the Paris Agreement.

labor.summary: Barclays employs approximately 83,000 people globally. Multiple rounds of significant workforce reductions have occurred. No major documented systemic labor violations have been identified as a primary concern.

environmental.summary: Rainforest Action Network's annual Banking on Climate Chaos report has ranked Barclays as Europe's largest fossil fuel financier every year since the report's inception — providing approximately $167 billion in fossil fuel financing from 2016 through 2023. ShareAction and other investor advocacy organizations have consistently found Barclays's climate commitments inadequate relative to Paris Agreement targets.

political.summary: The Qatar fundraising case — in which Barclays avoided a UK government bailout during the 2008 financial crisis by raising capital from Qatar's sovereign wealth fund through arrangements prosecutors alleged involved improper payments — was investigated by the UK Serious Fraud Office. The SFO brought criminal charges against Barclays and four executives in 2017. The cases were dropped following judicial rulings. The US DOJ civil resolution remained.

legal.summary: June 2012: Barclays became the first major bank fined for LIBOR manipulation — $290 million. June 2016: Barclays paid $100 million to 44 US states for LIBOR-related conduct. March 2022: Barclays agreed to pay $361 million to the DOJ for alleged improper payments to Qatari investors. August 2016: Barclays agreed to pay $2 billion to the DOJ for selling toxic mortgage-backed securities. Total US regulatory settlements exceed $2.5 billion.

tax.summary: In 2012, Barclays was forced to disclose two tax avoidance schemes after HMRC challenged them — paying approximately £500 million in backdated taxes and penalties.

executives.summary: CEO Bob Diamond resigned in July 2012 following the LIBOR fine. Current CEO CS Venkatakrishnan leads Barclays. No Barclays executive was criminally convicted — the SFO's individual cases were dropped on procedural grounds.

connections.summary: Barclays' LIBOR manipulation was part of an industry-wide scandal that implicated Deutsche Bank, UBS, Citigroup, JPMorgan Chase, and others — collectively paying over $9 billion in LIBOR-related fines globally. Barclays was first and its settlement established the template for others.

allegations.disclaimer: The following are allegations and unproven claims documented in credible sources but not adjudicated.
allegations.summary: The SFO's criminal charges alleged that the Qatari payments were structured as 'advisory service agreements' for services never actually rendered. Barclays has denied the characterization.

health_record.summary: Barclays's fossil fuel financing — $167 billion since the Paris Agreement — provides the capital for energy infrastructure that climate science documents will contribute to global warming beyond the 1.5°C target. The health consequences of climate change are documented by WHO and IPCC as among the most significant global health threats.

cheaper: UK building societies and credit unions — member-owned, not-for-profit; no LIBOR manipulation, no fossil fuel financing at Barclays's scale
healthier: Triodos Bank — certified B Corp; publishes full loan book; no fossil fuel financing by policy
diy: Check Barclays's current fossil fuel financing policies at shareaction.org
Move your account to a bank with documented fossil fuel exclusion policies

timeline.event: Barclays raises emergency capital from Qatar and Abu Dhabi; avoids UK government bailout; payments later alleged as improper
June: $290M LIBOR settlement — first major bank fined; CEO Bob Diamond resigns. February: HMRC forces disclosure of two Barclays tax avoidance schemes; ~£500M in backdated taxes paid.
$2B DOJ settlement for mortgage-backed securities fraud. $100M additional LIBOR settlement with 44 US states.
UK SFO brings criminal charges against Barclays and four executives over 2008 Qatar fundraising
SFO criminal case against Barclays dropped following Court of Appeal ruling on SFO procedures
$361M DOJ civil resolution for Qatar improper payments
Barclays ranked largest European fossil fuel financier for eighth consecutive year

community_impact.displacement: Communities near fossil fuel projects financed by Barclays's $167 billion in energy lending absorb environmental and health impacts from extraction and combustion.
community_impact.price_illusion: Barclays presents its fossil fuel financing as prudent capital allocation. Climate science published by the IEA and IPCC documents that investments in new fossil fuel infrastructure are incompatible with limiting warming to 1.5°C.
community_impact.tax_math: The HMRC tax avoidance schemes — for which Barclays paid £500 million in backdated taxes — involved structured financial transactions designed to generate artificial tax losses. The LIBOR and mortgage securities settlements have generated US tax deductions.
community_impact.wealth_velocity: Barclays is publicly traded on the London Stock Exchange. Revenue from fossil fuel financing transactions — which fund infrastructure contributing to climate change — flows to Barclays shareholders.

who_benefited.how: Avoided UK government bailout through Qatar capital; LIBOR manipulation revenue; mortgage securities fee income; fossil fuel financing fees

who_paid.how: Overcharged on interest rates by manipulated benchmark affecting trillions in financial contracts
Heat, flooding, air quality, displacement from climate change that Barclays-financed fossil fuel infrastructure contributes to

cost_absorption.the_gap: Barclays was the first major bank fined for LIBOR manipulation. It paid $2 billion for mortgage fraud. It paid $361 million for what the DOJ called improper payments to the investors who funded its 2008 survival. The UK's SFO charged it with fraud and then dropped the case on a procedural ruling. It has provided $167 billion in fossil fuel financing since the Paris Agreement. It is Europe's largest fossil fuel financier. Its net zero target is 2050.
